refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, IsRetired FROM silver.DeleteWhereTarget WHERE IsRetired <> 1;
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, IsRetired FROM silver.DeleteWhereTarget ), logical AS ( SELECT ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, IsRetired FROM import WHERE IsRetired <> 1 OR IsRetired IS NULL ), final AS ( SELECT ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, IsRetired FROM logical ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both SELECTs read from silver.DeleteWhereTarget only

output_columns.summary: Both return ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, IsRetired with matching types

joins.summary: No joins applicable; both queries are simple SELECTs from single source table

filters.summary: WHERE clauses are semantically different: extracted uses 'IsRetired <> 1' while refactored uses 'IsRetired <> 1 OR IsRetired IS NULL'. The refactored version includes additional NULL handling logic not present in extracted baseline

aggregation_grain.summary: No aggregation; both queries return row-level detail

issues.message: The refactored WHERE clause adds 'OR IsRetired IS NULL' condition not present in extracted SQL. While this is more semantically correct for handling NULL values in a BIT column, it represents a functional difference from the extracted baseline that would produce different result sets if NULL values exist
